data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 33d0 "Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one production path"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/59ef/822a/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, repo-corroborated this pass.

REPO VERIFICATION (this pass): apply_outbound.py confirms `labels_applied: list[str] = []` (L245), appended as `f"+{label_name}"`/`f"-{label_name}"` (L257/L260) and emitted in result_payload as `"labels_applied": labels_applied` (L389), whereas `comments_applied`/`links_applied` are ints (L278/L325, payload L390/L391). The no-op skip-guard at L372-378 uses TRUTHINESS (`not labels_applied`), NOT `== 0`. The production batch path (batch_dispatch.py update_one) carries NO per-sub-op counter today, so when 33d0 collapses onto the surviving path its parity gate must MINT the per-sub-op result representation for the survivor. Parent AC and 2359 cite int-valued inbound `links_applied` as the parity model.

RESOLVED (not re-filed): the prior 33d0↔2359 ordering-violation / which-path-survives mode rested on the absence of a sequencing edge. Sibling 2359 now declares `depends_on 33d0` (confirmed in 2359 deps; "Sequencing: depends on story D … so the counts come from one path"). 33d0 collapses to ONE production path FIRST, then 2359 instruments the single survivor — the same-region co-edit of batch_dispatch.py/applier.py is sequenced, not concurrent. Ordering + implicit-shared-state read benign.

ONE high-confidence finding remains that the depends_on edge does NOT resolve — symmetric counterpart of the finding filed on 2359; recorded here for this pairing from 33d0's side:

FINDING — conflicting assumptions: 33d0 owns the surviving applier's per-sub-op result payload, in which `labels_applied` is list-valued while `links_applied`/`comments_applied` are ints; downstream sibling 2359's `computed > 0 && applied == 0` count-canary assumes a uniform integer count, and 33d0 fixes no canonical label representation for the single survivor.
- finding: 33d0 What ("Retarget the existing typed-leaf tests at the surviving production path") plus its parity gate ("assert the surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did — fields, comments, labels, links, parent") make 33d0 the owner of the surviving applier's per-sub-op result payload. Because the production batch path has no counters today, 33d0 must establish that payload's shape for the survivor. The typed-leaf candidate shape it would carry over is NOT uniformly count-shaped (`labels_applied` list[str]; links/comments ints). Sibling 2359 (depends_on 33d0) then surfaces `labels_applied` in the SAME count-and-invariant frame as links/comments and defines a `computed > 0 && applied == 0` canary citing int parity (inbound `links_applied`). 33d0 nowhere states the canonical label representation for the single surviving payload, so the survivor 2359 instruments is handed an ambiguous label signal: keep the list (2359's `applied == 0` predicate is ill-typed over a list — `[]` ≠ `0`, a non-empty list is truthy) or flip to an int (breaks any exact-list label assertion 33d0 just retargeted). Either way a shared contract is left unowned at the seam between THIS child (owns the survivor's payload) and 2359 (consumes it for the canary). The depends_on edge sequences the work but does not by itself decide the label representation.
  - criteria: 33d0 AC1 (exactly one applier path; retired path deleted), 33d0 AC2 (behavioral parity — surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did, before deletion), 33d0 AC3 (single multi-sub-op UPDATE — fields+comments+labels+links+parent — asserting each sub-op took effect); cross-checked against 2359 AC1 (surface labels_applied) + 2359 AC2/AC5 (computed>0 && applied==0 count canary); parent AC "Outbound application is collapsed to a single production path" (33d0) vs parent AC "A `computed > 0 && applied == 0` sub-op condition fails loudly" (2359)
  - location: 33d0 What/Scope (retarget typed-leaf tests; parity gate over the surviving payload); src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_outbound.py L245 (`labels_applied: list[str] = []`), L257/L260 (append `f"+{label_name}"`/`f"-{label_name}"`), L389 (`"labels_applied": labels_applied`) vs L278/L325 (`comments_applied`/`links_applied` int), L372-378 no-op guard (truthiness `not labels_applied`, NOT `== 0`); src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_inbound.py inbound `links_applied: int` (the parity model 2359 cites); src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/batch_dispatch.py update_one (production batch path applies comments/links/labels but tracks NO per-sub-op counter); src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/applier.py RECON batch_outcome (emits only action/key/error)
  - evidence: repo confirms the surviving-applier candidate payload carries `labels_applied` as list[str] (L245/L389) and links/comments_applied as ints (L325/L278); the production batch path carries NO per-sub-op counter, so 33d0's parity gate must mint the label representation for the survivor; the existing no-op guard 33d0 may carry over uses truthiness (`not labels_applied`), not a `== 0` count; 2359 (depends_on 33d0) then layers a count-based `computed>0 && applied==0` invariant on that very key citing an int parity model
  - scenarios: (a) 33d0 lands the survivor keeping `labels_applied` as a list → 2359's `applied == 0` canary over labels mis-fires or never fires (`[]` ≠ `0`; non-empty list truthy) → the silent-no-op guarantee silently fails for the labels sub-op specifically even while links/comments are covered → reinstates a labels-shaped instance of the bug-3f04 class. (b) 33d0 (or 2359) normalizes `labels_applied` to an int → any exact-list label assertion 33d0 retargeted breaks → parent "no capability regresses; full reconciler suite green" (33d0 AC4) regresses. (c) 33d0 leaves the representation unstated → the surviving-applier payload contract (33d0) and the canary (2359) disagree on which field/shape the invariant reads.
  - impact: 33d0's parity gate and retargeted tests cannot be checked off consistently with 2359's count-canary unless 33d0 fixes the surviving payload's label representation; a silent risk to parent SC "a silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary instead of passing green" (holds for links/comments but possibly NOT labels) and to parent "full reconciler suite green" (33d0 AC4) if the list key is flipped.
  - checklist_item: cross-child modes — conflicting assumptions (shared outbound-payload label representation between 33d0's surviving applier and 2359's count-canary)
  - suggested_fix: in 33d0, fix the surviving consolidated applier's per-sub-op result representation explicitly — keep the existing list-valued `labels_applied` AND expose a derived count (e.g. `labels_applied_count` / `len(labels_applied)`) so any exact-list test survives while an integer count exists for the invariant — and state that one representation in 33d0's parity/AC so 2359's `computed > 0 && applied == 0` predicate is well-typed over labels by construction across the single production path. Also leave the surviving dispatch loop structured so 2359 can thread per-sub-op counts in without re-architecting it.

Modes that read BENIGN (not filed): ordering violation — RESOLVED via 2359 depends_on 33d0. Implicit shared state — the batch_dispatch.py/applier.py co-edit with 2359 is sequenced by that same edge (33d0 lands first); residual is only that 33d0 should leave the surviving dispatch loop structured so 2359 can thread counters in — folded into the suggested_fix above. Dependency gap (on inputs) — 33d0 is pure outbound-code consolidation needing no schema/fixture input, so unlike 822a/59ef (which correctly declare depends_on fe57) its lack of an input dep is appropriate. Scope overlap — 33d0 AC3 (outbound-only combined multi-sub-op UPDATE asserting each sub-op took effect) vs 822a AC1 (bidirectional inbound↔outbound round-trip) vs 2359 AC2 (computed>0&&applied==0 canary) are distinct intents over the same production path; 33d0 declares telemetry OUT (→story E/2359) consistently. Consumer impact — once 33d0 collapses to one path, 8937 AC3 / 822a AC1 (which drive "the production path") target the survivor by construction; benign given 33d0 lands first per the dependency graph. Residual references — 33d0 explicitly deletes the dead typed leaf AND its now-misleading tests (33d0 AC1 + the parity-before-delete gate), so it does not strand references; siblings 8937/fe57/59ef key on the inbound snapshot/fetch surface, not the deleted typed leaf.

The roster-level parent anti-change-detector-guardrail matter is a G3 coverage question (and is owned by story B/8937 per the parent AC's explicit "(owned by story B)" clause) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not addressed here.
